description: Add undo system for mandate turns (marshal, recruit, betray). Store a snapshot of gameState when each player's mandate turn begins, add an undo button (circular arrow icon) to the ActionPanel, and restore the snapshot on click. The confirm/end-turn button finalizes (clears snapshot).
steps: In src/components/Icons.tsx: Add a new UndoIcon component - a circular arrow (counterclockwise) SVG icon following the same IconProps pattern as other icons.
In src/store/gameStore.ts interface GameStore: Add 'undoMandateState: GameState | null' field. Add 'doUndoMandate: () => void' action. Add 'saveMandateSnapshot: () => void' helper (or inline).
In the store implementation (create<GameStore> initial values): Add undoMandateState: null.

In doChooseMandateTile and doLotoChooseActualMandate: After computing `ns`, if ns.marshalMandateActive || ns.recruitMandateActive || ns.betrayMandateActive is true, save a deep copy of `ns` (the state at the start of the mandate turn for the first player) as undoMandateState. Use JSON parse/stringify for deep clone.

When mandate player advances to next player (in doSkipMarshalTurn, doSkipRecruitTurn, doSkipBetrayTurn when the mandate is still active for the next player): Save a new snapshot of the current state as undoMandateState before the player starts their turn.

Implement doUndoMandate: Restore gameState from undoMandateState, reset moveMode/buildFortressMode/recruitMode/betrayMode/moveFrom/selectedFigures to their defaults, and keep undoMandateState as-is (so the player can undo again if they make more changes).

When mandate turn is finalized (doSkipMarshalTurn when marshalMandateActive becomes false, doSkipRecruitTurn when recruitMandateActive becomes false, doSkipBetrayTurn, doBetraySelectFigure when mandate ends): Clear undoMandateState to null.

In src/components/ActionPanel.tsx: Import UndoIcon from './Icons'. In the marshal-active, recruit-active, and betray-active sections, add an undo button before or next to the end-turn button. Style it as a small circular button with the UndoIcon. Only show it when undoMandateState is not null. Wire it to doUndoMandate from the store.
Add doUndoMandate to the destructured values from useGameStore at the top of ActionPanel.
acceptance_criteria: UndoIcon component exists in Icons.tsx with a circular arrow design
undoMandateState field exists in the store interface and implementation
doUndoMandate action restores the snapshot state and resets UI modes
Undo button appears in marshal, recruit, and betray mandate sections
Undo button is only visible when undoMandateState is not null
End-turn/confirm actions clear undoMandateState when mandate ends
